debug_info.goal:
R : Type ?u.28136
inst✝¹ : CommRing R
S : Type ?u.28142
inst✝ : CommRing S
f : R →+* S
hf : Surjective ⇑f
⊢ ∀ (x y : R ⧸ ker f),
    (@GlimpseOfLean.kerLift R inst✝¹ S inst✝ f) (x * y) =
      (@GlimpseOfLean.kerLift R inst✝¹ S inst✝ f) x * (@GlimpseOfLean.kerLift R inst✝¹ S inst✝ f) y

intro x y
      have h : kerLift f (x * y) = kerLift f x * kerLift f y := by
        -- Use the universal property of the quotient ring to show that the multiplication is preserved.
        have h₁ : (x * y : R ⧸ ker f) = (x : R ⧸ ker f) * (y : R ⧸ ker f) := rfl
        -- Use the property of the quotient ring to simplify the expression.
        have h₂ : kerLift f (x * y) = kerLift f x * kerLift f y := by
          -- Use the fact that the lift of the product is the product of the lifts.
          simp [kerLift, Ideal.Quotient.lift_map_eq, h₁]
          <;>
          simp_all [ringHom.map_mul]
          <;>
          aesop
        exact h₂
      exact h
  }

intro x y
      have h₁ : kerLift f (x * y) = f ((x : R) * (y : R)) := by
        rw [show x * y = Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) ((x : R) * (y : R)) by

          rw [kerLift_mk]
        have h₄ : kerLift f y = f (y : R) := by
          rw [show y = Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (y : R) by rfl]
          rw [kerLift_mk]
        rw [h₃, h₄]
      have h₃ : f ((x : R) * (y : R)) = f (x : R) * f (y : R) := by
        apply f.map_mul
      rw [h₁, h₃, h₂]

    map_one' := by

    map_add' := by

intro x y
      have h1 : kerLift f (x * y) = f (x.repr * y.repr) := by
        calc
          kerLift f (x * y) = Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f (fun x ↦ by simp [Ideal.mem_ker]) (x * y) := rfl
          _ = f (x * y).repr := by
            -- Use the property of the lift to simplify the expression
            simp [Ideal.Quotient.lift_apply, Ideal.Quotient.ext_iff, Ideal.mem_ker] at *
            <;>
            aesop
          _ = f (x.repr * y.repr) := by
            -- Use the property of the quotient ring to simplify the expression
            have h2 : (x * y : R ⧸ ker f).repr = x.repr * y.repr := by
              -- Prove that the representative of the product is the product of the representatives
              simp [Ideal.Quotient.ext_iff, mul_add, add_mul, mul_comm, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc]
              <;>
              (try ring_nf) <;>
              (try simp_all [Ideal.mem_ker]) <;>
              (try aesop)
            rw [h2]
            <;>
            simp_all [Ideal.Quotient.lift_apply, Ideal.Quotient.ext_iff, Ideal.mem_ker]
      have h2 : kerLift f x * kerLift f y = f (x.repr) * f (y.repr) := by
        calc
          kerLift f x * kerLift f y = (Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f (fun x ↦ by simp [Ideal.mem_ker]) x) * (Ideal.Quotient.lift (ker f) f (fun x ↦ by simp [Ideal.mem_ker]) y) := rfl
          _ = f x.repr * f y.repr := by
            -- Use the property of the lift to simplify the expression
            simp [Ideal.Quotient.lift_apply, Ideal.Quotient.ext_iff, Ideal.mem_ker] at *
            <;>
            aesop
      calc
        kerLift f (x * y) = f (x.repr * y.repr) := h1
        _ = f (x.repr) * f (y.repr) := by
          -- Use the ring homomorphism property to split the product
          rw [f.map_mul]
        _ = kerLift f x * kerLift f y := by
          -- Use the previously derived expression for kerLift f x * kerLift f y
          rw [h2]
  }

intro x y
      have h₁ : (kerLift f (x * y) : S) = f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) ((x : R / ker f) * (y : R / ker f))) := by
        simp [kerLift, Ideal.Quotient.lift_apply]
      have h₂ : (kerLift f x : S) = f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (x : R / ker f)) := by
        simp [kerLift, Ideal.Quotient.lift_apply]
      have h₃ : (kerLift f y : S) = f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (y : R / ker f)) := by
        simp [kerLift, Ideal.Quotient.lift_apply]
      calc
        (kerLift f (x * y) : S) = f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) ((x : R / ker f) * (y : R / ker f))) := by rw [h₁]
        _ = f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (x : R / ker f) * Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (y : R / ker f)) := by
          simp [Ideal.Quotient.mul_def]
          <;>
          simp_all [RingHom.map_mul]
          <;>
          try norm_num
          <;>
          try aesop
        _ = f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (x : R / ker f)) * f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (y : R / ker f)) := by
          apply f.map_mul
        _ = (kerLift f x : S) * (kerLift f y : S) := by
          simp_all [kerLift, Ideal.Quotient.lift_apply]
  }

intro x y

intro x y
      have h_mul : kerLift f (x * y) = (kerLift f x) * (kerLift f y) := by
        -- Since `kerLift f` is constructed as `Ideal.Quotient.lift`, which is a ring homomorphism,
        -- it automatically preserves multiplication.
        exact Ideal.Quotient.map_mul (ker f) (f) x y
      exact h_mul
  }

intro x y
      simp [kerLift, Ideal.Quotient.lift, Ideal.Quotient.map_mul]

      <;>

  }

have h₁ : Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (surjInv hf x) = x := by
        exact rightInverse_surjInv hf x
      have h₂ : kerLift f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (surjInv hf x)) = f (surjInv hf x) := by
        rw [kerLift_mk]
      have h₃ : f (surjInv hf x) = x := by
        have h₄ : f (surjInv hf x) = x := by
          apply Function.rightInverse_of_surjective
          <;> simp_all [surjInv]
        exact h₄
      calc
        kerLift f x = kerLift f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (surjInv hf x)) := by
          rw [h₁]
        _ = f (surjInv hf x) := by rw [kerLift_mk]
        _ = x := by rw [h₃]
    map_mul' x y := by
      have h₁ : kerLift f (x * y) = f (RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f (x * y)) := by
        rw [kerLift_mk]
      have h₂ : kerLift f x * kerLift f y = f (RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f x) * f (RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f y) := by
        simp [kerLift_mk, RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.map_mul]
      have h₃ : f (RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f (x * y)) = f (RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f x) * f (RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f y) := by
        have h₄ : RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f (x * y) = RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f x * RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f y := by
          -- Prove that the lift of the product is the product of the lifts
          have h₅ : ∃ (a b : R), x = Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) a ∧ y = Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) b := by
            have h₆ : Function.Surjective (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f)) := by
              exact Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
            obtain ⟨a, ha⟩ := h₆ x
            obtain ⟨b, hb⟩ := h₆ y
            refine' ⟨a, b, _⟩
            <;> simp_all [ha, hb]
          obtain ⟨a, b, ha, hb⟩ := h₅
          have h₇ : x = Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) a := ha
          have h₈ : y = Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) b := hb
          have h₉ : RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f (x * y) = f a * f b := by
            calc
              RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f (x * y) = RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) a * Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) b) := by rw [h₇, h₈]
              _ = RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) (a * b)) := by
                simp [Ideal.Quotient.mk_mul_mk]
              _ = f (a * b) := by rw [kerLift_mk]
              _ = f a * f b := by rw [map_mul]
              _ = f a * f b := by rfl
          have h₁₀ : RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f x * RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f y = (f a) * (f b) := by
            calc
              RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f x * RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f y =RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) a) * RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f (Ideal.Quotient.mk (ker f) b) := by rw [h₇, h₈]
              _ = f a * f b := by
                simp [kerLift_mk]
              _ = (f a) * (f b) := by rfl
          rw [h₉, h₁₀]
        rw [h₄]
        <;> simp [map_mul]
      calc
        kerLift f (x * y) = f (RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f (x * y)) := by rw [h₁]
        _ = f (RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f x) * f (RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.toFun f y) := by rw [h₃]
        _ = kerLift f x * kerLift f y := by
          simp_all [kerLift_mk]
          <;>
          try simp_all [RingHomClass.kerLiftClass.map_mul]
          <;>
          try ring_nf at *
          <;>
          try aesop
    }

intro x y
      have h₁ : (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) (x * y) = (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) x * (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) y := by
        have h₂ : (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) (x * y) = (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) (x * y) := rfl
        have h₃ : (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) x * (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) y = (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) (x * y) := by
          calc
            (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) x * (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) y = (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) x * (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) y := by rfl
            _ = (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) (x * y) := by
              -- Use the fact that `kerLift f` is a ring homomorphism to show that it preserves multiplication
              have h₄ : (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) (x * y) = (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) x * (kerLift f : R ⧸ ker f →+* S) y := by
                -- Use the property of ring homomorphisms to preserve multiplication
                apply map_mul
              rw [h₄]
        rw [h₃]
      exact h₁
  }

intro x y
      simp [kerLift, Ideal.Quotient.lift_mul]
      <;>

sorry

intro x y
      -- Use the fact that the map is a homomorphism to show multiplicativity
      have h₁ : kerLift f (x * y) = kerLift f x * kerLift f y := by
        -- Since kerLift f is a ring homomorphism, it respects multiplication
        have h₂ : kerLift f (x * y) = kerLift f x * kerLift f y := by
          -- Use the property of kerLift to show this
          have h₃ : kerLift f (x * y) = kerLift f x * kerLift f y := by
            -- Since kerLift f is a ring homomorphism, it respects multiplication
            have h₄ : kerLift f (x * y) = kerLift f x * kerLift f y := by
              -- Use the property of kerLift to show this
              simpa [Ideal.Quotient.lift_mk] using (Ideal.Quotient.map_mul f)
            exact h₄
          exact h₃
        exact h₂
      exact h₁
  }
